title: plan.lock.json
description: Machine-readable mirror of plan.md emitted by /vision and consumed by /compose. Authoritative for stack, data model, features, formula picks, deps, and escalation budget. plan.md remains the human narrative.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes; /decompose refuses unknown versions. v2 added mustHaves[], successMetric, coverage[], concerns[] for the plan-stage coverage gate.

mustHaves.description: The product must-haves /vision lifted from vision.md §3, each with a stable ID (M1, M2, ...). Every entry must appear in coverage[] mapped to >=1 feature, else /vision flips incomplete=true. The set the forward-coverage gate checks against.

phase.description: Phase this must-have is assigned to (matches a phases[].id). Optional; default 1 when omitted. A must-have tagged to a future phase is a deliberate deferral (decompose verifier C), not a coverage gap.

successMetric.description: The vision.md §8 success metric decomposed into observable steps (S1, S2, ...). The success-metric oracle (bfo.5) maps each step to >=1 covered feature.

phases.description: Optional ordered build phases for phased apps (epic autonomous-build-0ms). A lock with no phases[] (and no phase tags) is a single-phase plan and behaves exactly as today, so this field is additive: schemaVersion STAYS 2. The pipeline JIT-decomposes one phase at a time, so phase isolation is the orchestration loop pausing to replan/decompose the next slice, not a graph gate. Cross-phase dependencies must point backward only (phase N+1 may depend on N, never the reverse). See docs/PHASED_BUILD_PROPOSAL.md.

id.description: Stable 1-based phase number. Phase 1 is the walking skeleton — the minimal features that make successMetric.steps[] run end-to-end.

goal.description: One-line goal: what this phase delivers as an independently-shippable slice.

status.description: Lifecycle: planned (not yet decomposed) | active (currently decomposing/building) | built (drained + frozen). /replan freezes phases with status=built and re-derives the rest.

provisional.description: True for a later-phase sketch (goal + rough must-have assignment) that /replan firms up when the phase is reached. Phase 1 is provisional:false — fully decided and decompose-ready.

stack.description: Tech stack decisions, keyed by layer. Each value is the chosen tool and the one-line justification.

featureOrder.description: Ordered list of features. Each feature names one or more formulas to pour and the variable bindings.

tier.description: Authoritative build-order tier derived from the formula picks (deriveFeatureTier, epic autonomous-build-onv). Ordering: foundational (app-skeleton/observability bootstrap — the scaffolding the whole tree depends on) < platform (shared services/infra: tenancy, auth[nz], audit, IaC, migrations, secrets, composer grammar) < feature (product features: CRUD, gRPC, integrations, jobs — the default) < enforcement (concern-enforcement + acceptance/e2e gates, built last). An entry pouring multiple formulas takes the most-foundational tier among them. /decompose wires the cross-epic ordering edges from this (and derives a fallback from the formula category for old locks without it).

requires.description: Optional finer cross-feature ordering vision can determine: featureOrder[].name entries this feature must be built after, beyond the coarse tier ordering. Empty by default; reserved.

vars.description: Variable bindings passed to bd mol pour --var k=v. Values may be strings, numbers, booleans, or arrays of those.

phase.description: Phase this feature belongs to (matches a phases[].id). Optional; default 1 when omitted — a lock with no phases is a single-phase plan. /decompose --phase N pours only the features tagged for phase N.

coverage.description: Forward-coverage map: each must-have (by mustHaveId) to the feature(s) that deliver it and HOW. /vision asserts every mustHaves[].id appears here with >=1 feature; an uncovered must-have becomes a blocking openQuestion (incomplete=true).

features.description: featureOrder[].name entries that deliver this must-have.

how.description: HOW the feature(s) deliver the must-have — falsifiable evidence, not a bare link. Mirrors the decompose anti-vagueness invariant.

concerns.description: One decided entry per applicable cross-cutting concern (data-model, authn, authz, secrets, data-lifecycle, error-handling, observability, external-integrations, perf-envelope, abuse-surface). concernId is validated against docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md by /vision, NOT enumerated here — vocabulary edits never force a schema bump. Every applicable concern resolves to addressed (with evidence) or excluded (with reason); silence is the failure mode this prevents.

concernId.description: A stable concernId from docs/PLAN_CONCERNS.md. Free string here; the vocabulary lives in that doc, not the schema.

evidence.description: Falsifiable evidence the concern is handled (a feature name, formula, tenet, gate, or stack pin). Required when status=addressed.

reason.description: One-line reason the concern does not apply. Required when status=excluded.

nfrs.description: Non-functional requirements with MEASURABLE targets, lifted from vision.md §6 constraints (performance, security, privacy, compliance, data-residency, availability, ...). Unlike concerns[] (decide-and-cite prose evidence), each NFR is FIRST-CLASS: /decompose pours a dedicated enforcement bead whose acceptance criterion is derived from `target` and whose test is seeded from `verify`, and gates that every NFR maps to >=1 enforcement bead (NEEDS-FIX otherwise). This is what keeps 'data stays in my region' from evaporating into an advisory tenet sentence. Optional + may be omitted so adding it does not force a schemaVersion bump; /vision populates it from §6.

statement.description: The requirement in product terms, e.g. 'All user data stored in EU regions only.'

target.description: The MEASURABLE/testable bar, e.g. 'p99 < 200ms at 100 concurrent' or 'no row written outside eu-* regions'. Not a vibe.

verify.description: How the enforcement bead's test checks the target — the test-plan seed.

crossFeatureDependencies.description: Each entry produces a `bd dep add <blocked> <blocker>` call after the pour.

blocked.description: Feature name (matches featureOrder[].name) or specific bead ID.

blocker.description: Feature name (matches featureOrder[].name) or specific bead ID.

openQuestions.description: Product/scope questions /vision could not resolve. Any with blockingCompose=true must be answered before /compose runs. Tech ambiguity does NOT go here — it routes to agentConsults.

agentConsults.description: Decisions /vision made via the 3-agent off-stack consult (architect / reviewer / counter-arguer). Each entry is one decided question. Not surfaced to the human during planning; reviewed at the plan.md gate.

incomplete.description: True iff openQuestions contains any entry with blockingCompose=true. /compose refuses to run when this is true.
